perturbation.original_text: "EXCLUSIVE CATEGORY" means those product and/or service categories identified on Exhibit A.
perturbation.changed_text: "EXCLUSIVE CATEGORY" means any product and/or service categories that either party chooses to designate as exclusive at any time during the term of this agreement, subject to mutual written agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying the definition of 'EXCLUSIVE CATEGORY,' which was originally definitively specified by 'Exhibit A' by the insertion of 'any product and/or service categories that either party chooses to designate as exclusive at any time during the term of this agreement, subject to mutual written agreement'. This allows flexibility that contradicts the definiteness of Exhibit A, and also introduces a contradiction with the 'SECTION 2.6 EXCLUSIVE SPONSORSHIP' clause, which limits Snap's ability to grant sponsorship rights to third parties in the Exclusive Category, now ambiguous and subject to change. The contradiction arises from the shift in control and definiteness of the 'Exclusive Category.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the Term, Snap will not grant any third party any right to sponsor any products or services in the Exclusive Category on or through the Snap Web Site. For the avoidance of doubt, the parties acknowledge that the foregoing restriction applies only to persistent sponsorship placement as judged by Sponsor at its discretion, and not to run-of-site banner advertisements or other rotating promotional placements.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'EXCLUSIVE CATEGORY' from being definitively specified in 'Exhibit A' to being subject to either party's designation with mutual written agreement. This introduces flexibility and ambiguity. The contradicted text, 'During the Term, Snap will not grant any third party any right to sponsor any products or services in the Exclusive Category,' relies on a clear and fixed definition of 'Exclusive Category.' The changed definition creates a strong contradiction because Snap could now unilaterally alter what constitutes the 'Exclusive Category,' potentially impacting the third-party sponsorship rights outlined in the contradicted text. This makes the restriction on Snap's ability to grant third-party rights uncertain and dependent on future agreements, undermining the original definiteness. This fits the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' and 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definitions.

perturbation.original_text: The Term shall commence on the date of this Agreement and, unless earlier terminated or extended as provided below, shall end as of December 31, 2000.
perturbation.changed_text: The Term shall commence on the date of this Agreement and, unless earlier terminated or extended as provided below, shall automatically renew for successive one-year periods unless either party provides written notice of termination at least 30 days prior to the end of the then-current term. However, United Airlines, Inc., at its sole discretion, can choose to terminate this agreement at any time with no prior notice.
perturbation.explanation: The insertion of 'shall automatically renew for successive one-year periods unless either party provides written notice of termination at least 30 days prior to the end of the then-current term. However, United Airlines, Inc., at its sole discretion, can choose to terminate this agreement at any time with no prior notice' adds an auto-renewal clause and special privilege for United Airlines, Inc. to terminate at any time with no prior notice. This contradicts the original termination conditions specified in Section 4.2, creating uncertainty. It is unclear under what conditions Snap can end the agreement. Moreover, it introduces an unfair termination clause, favouring United Airlines, Inc. over Snap.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Either party may terminate the Term upon not less than sixty (60) days prior written notice to the other party of any breach hereof by such other party. In addition, either party may terminate the Term immediately upon written notice in the event that the other party shall fail to do business in the normal course or become subject to any bankruptcy, assignment for creditors, corporate dissolution or similar proceeding.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an auto-renewal clause and grants United Airlines, Inc. the right to terminate the agreement at any time without prior notice. This directly contradicts the original text (which stated the contract would end on a specific date) and the contradicted text, which stipulates that either party may terminate the term with 60 days' written notice or immediately under specific conditions (breach, bankruptcy, etc.). The changed text creates uncertainty as to when and how Snap can terminate the agreement, making it a strong in-text contradiction under the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: During the Term, and for a period of five (5) years thereafter, the receiving party will not disclose to others or use for any purpose of its own, other than in performance of this Agreement, any Confidential Information of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: During the Term, and for a period of five (5) years thereafter, the receiving party will not disclose to others or use for any purpose of its own, other than in performance of this Agreement, any Confidential Information of the other party, except if the receiving party deems such disclosure necessary for business development purposes. Any disclosure for business development purposes will not be subject to the confidentiality obligations outlined in this section.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'except if the receiving party deems such disclosure necessary for business development purposes. Any disclosure for business development purposes will not be subject to the confidentiality obligations outlined in this section', the change significantly weakens the confidentiality clause. Originally, the text strictly prohibited disclosure of Confidential Information for five years post-term. The altered text allows a party to disclose confidential information if they deem it 'necessary for business development purposes', effectively negating the confidentiality obligations. This introduces a clear contradiction, as the original clause aims to protect confidential information, while the revised clause provides a broad exception undermining that protection. This creates uncertainty around what information is truly protected and under what circumstances it can be disclosed, leading to potential disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the Term, and for a period of five (5) years thereafter, the receiving party will not disclose to others or use for any purpose of its own, other than in performance of this Agreement, any Confidential Information of the other party. Each party shall take, at a minimum, measures consistent with those taken to protect its own similar types of Confidential Information (and in any event, at least reasonable measures) to protect the other's Confidential Information against disclosures prohibited by this Agreement. Each party acknowledges that its breach of the provisions of this Section 8.1 [CONFIDENTIALITY] will result in immediate and irreparable harm to the other and that money damages alone would be inadequate to compensate such party. Therefore, in the event of a breach of this Section 8.1 [CONFIDENTIALITY] by either party the other party may, in addition to other remedies, immediately obtain and enforce injunctive relief prohibiting the breach or threatened breach or compelling specific performance. Notwithstanding any other provision of this Agreement, the restrictions set forth in this Section 8.1 [CONFIDENTIALITY] shall not apply to any information that: (i) is in or enters the public domain through no fault of the receiving party; (ii) is disclosed to the receiving party by a third party legally entitled to make such disclosure; (iii) is independently developed by the receiving party without reference to any Confidential Information of the other party; or (iv) is required to be disclosed by applicable law, regulation or order of any governmental authority; provided, that in such event, the receiving party shall provide the disclosing party with prior notice that is reasonable in the circumstances of such disclosure and shall use reasonable efforts to cooperate with the disclosing party to minimize the extent and scope of such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the confidentiality obligations, allowing disclosure for 'business development purposes,' which directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted section, which strictly prohibits disclosure of Confidential Information for five years post-term. The contradicted text outlines measures to protect confidential information and consequences for breach, while the changed text provides an exception that undermines that protection. This creates a clear and direct conflict and in-text contradiction because the confidentiality obligation is no longer absolute. This aligns with the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition, where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.
